is a professional [football](/wiki/Football_(soccer) "Football (soccer)") club from [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar"), founded in 1965 and a member of the [Gibraltar Football Association](/wiki/Gibraltar_Football_Association "Gibraltar Football Association") (GFA). The club share the [Victoria Stadium](/wiki/Victoria_Stadium_(Gibraltar) "Victoria Stadium (Gibraltar)") with all other teams in the territory.

["First Division 17/18 Results – Gibraltar Football Association"](https://web.archive.org/web/20180612141536/https://www.gibraltarfa.com/domestic-football/results/853340). Archived from [the original](https://www.gibraltarfa.com/domestic-football/results/853340) on 12 June 2018. Retrieved 7 May 2018.

**Glacis United Football Club** is a professional [football](/wiki/Football_(soccer) "Football (soccer)") club from [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar"), founded in 1965 and a member of the [Gibraltar Football Association](/wiki/Gibraltar_Football_Association "Gibraltar Football Association") (GFA). The club share the [Victoria Stadium](/wiki/Victoria_Stadium_(Gibraltar) "Victoria Stadium (Gibraltar)") with all other teams in the territory.

One of Gibraltar's most decorated teams, Glacis United currently compete in the [Gibraltar Football League](/wiki/Gibraltar_Football_League "Gibraltar Football League").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glacis_United_F.C.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Glacis United were formed in 1965 and immediately saw success as the dominant force in Gibraltarian football throughout the 1960s through to the 1980s, winning 9 consecutive titles from 1965 to 1966 to 1973–74 and further consecutive titles in the 1980s. However, the emergence of [Lincoln Red Imps](/wiki/Lincoln_Red_Imps_F.C. "Lincoln Red Imps F.C.") saw Glacis's dominance decline, and their 17th and final title to date came in the 1999–2000 season. Since then, the club have settled into mid-table in Gibraltar, with the club particularly struggling since the GFA joined [UEFA](/wiki/UEFA "UEFA") in 2013. The recruitment of Manuel Jimenez Perez in 2016, however, saw a more successful season with the club finishing 4th in the 2016–17 season, challenging [St Joseph's](/wiki/St_Joseph%27s_F.C. "St Joseph's F.C.") for 3rd place and [UEFA Europa League](/wiki/UEFA_Europa_League "UEFA Europa League") qualification. Manager Manuel Jimenez Perez left at the end of the season, and was replaced by Mariano Marcos Garcia on 9 July 2017. Juan Andrés Espinosa was announced as his assistant.

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Glacis_United_F.C.&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Honours")]

* **[Gibraltar Premier Division](/wiki/Gibraltar_Premier_Division "Gibraltar Premier Division"): 17**

:   :   1965–66, 1966–67, 1967–68, 1968–69, 1969–70, 1970–71, 1971–72, 1972–73, 1973–74, 1975–76, 1980–81, 1981–82, 1982–83, 1984–85, 1988–89, 1996–97, 1999–2000

* **[Rock Cup](/wiki/Rock_Cup "Rock Cup"): 5**

:   :   1974–75, 1980–81, 1981–82, 1996–97, 1997–98

* **[Pepe Reyes Cup](/wiki/Pepe_Reyes_Cup "Pepe Reyes Cup"): 2**

:   :   2000, 2005

* **[Gibraltar Division 2 Cup](/wiki/Gibraltar_Division_2_Cup "Gibraltar Division 2 Cup"): 2**

:   :   2005, 2007 *(won by Glacis United Reserve team)*
